hero.title: Office of the Vice President for External Affairs
hero.subtitle: Leading the University’s strategic partnerships, institutional branding, alumni relations, and global engagement in support of excellence in instruction, research, and community service.

overview.title: Overview
overview.intro: The Office of the Vice President for External Affairs (OVPEA) provides strategic leadership and coherent oversight of Angeles University Foundation’s externally oriented units, ensuring meaningful engagement with local, national, and international stakeholders in support of the University’s mission and development goals.
paragraphs: Since its establishment, the Angeles University Foundation (AUF) has been collaborating with external institutions and stakeholders to achieve its vision of becoming a center of excellence in instruction, research, and community service.

To further establish an institution-wide system that allows its academic and support units to establish meaningful partnerships with various agencies and groups at the local, national, and international levels for mutual benefits and in support of its mission and vision, the University established the Office of the Vice President for External Affairs (OVPEA).

The OVPEA is intended to provide strategic leadership and coherent oversight of units whose functions are externally oriented, ensuring strategic engagement with local, national, and international stakeholders in support of the institution’s mission and development goals.
The OVPEA ensures harmonized implementation of initiatives and programs for networking, linkage, and partnership, and adherence to the standards and policies of regulating and accrediting bodies.

visionMission.title: Vision, Mission & Goals
visionMission.vision: The Office of the Vice President for External Affairs envisions Angeles University Foundation as a globally recognized, socially responsive, and industry-engaged university, strengthened by strategic partnerships, a credible institutional brand, and an empowered alumni community in support of excellence in instruction, research, and community service.
visionMission.mission: The Office of the Vice President for External Affairs is committed to leading, coordinating, and sustaining the University’s external engagements by promoting meaningful partnerships, strengthening institutional branding and visibility, and cultivating lifelong alumni relations, thereby advancing the University’s mission, strategic goals, and global standing.

goals.heading: Build Strategic Partnerships
goals.body: Establish, manage, and sustain strategic local, national, and international partnerships with academic institutions, industry, government agencies, alumni, and other stakeholders that support instruction, research, and community engagement;

goals.heading: Develop Policies and Systems
goals.body: Formulate and implement policies, guidelines, and systems governing external relations, partnerships, and engagements to ensure coherence, accountability, compliance with regulatory and accrediting bodies, and alignment with the University’s strategic plan;

goals.heading: Strengthen Institutional Branding
goals.body: Strengthen the University’s public image, brand identity, and global visibility through coordinated communication, public relations, marketing initiatives, and strategic representation in national and international platforms;

goals.heading: Advance Alumni and Placement Programs
goals.body: Develop and sustain dynamic alumni relations and placement programs that promote alumni participation, career development, industry collaboration, philanthropy, and graduate employability;

goals.heading: Promote Internationalization
goals.body: Promote and coordinate international programs and initiatives, including academic exchanges, joint research, mobility programs, benchmarking, and global networks, in collaboration with academic and support units;

goals.heading: Generate Measurable Outcomes
goals.body: Generate measurable outcomes in external engagement that contribute to institutional reputation, quality assurance, accreditation readiness, and global rankings;

goals.heading: Adopt Innovative Practices
goals.body: Adopt innovative and data-informed practices to ensure high stakeholder satisfaction, continuous performance improvement, and responsiveness to emerging local and global opportunities.

milestones.title: Center for Global Education (CGE) Established
milestones.body: AUF supports internationalization or globalization, particularly the initiatives geared towards international or cross-border education. It is in this spirit that AUF launched the Center for Global Education (CGE) on June 22, 1997 which has the following objectives:
objectives: To promote the internationalization of the University’s quest for academic excellence, thus elevating the institution’s world-class status;
To forge linkages and collaboration with other institutions of higher learning in the Asia-Pacific region;
To provide relevant training programs to meet the manpower demands of business and industry;
To serve as the center in formulating global agenda that will address key issues and strategies on higher education in preparation for the next millennium; and
To provide the exchange of publications and academic information, faculty and students.

milestones.title: Evolution to International Affairs (IA)
milestones.body: Under the Office of the Vice President for Academic Affairs (OVPAA), the CGE evolved into the International Affairs (IA). The IA was established in the same spirit as that of the CGE, that is, to be responsible for the integration and implementation of the ethos of internationalization thrust of AUF. Specifically, it has the following objectives:
objectives: Network and establish partnerships with leading universities in the ASEAN region;
Improve the environment for international support services within AUF;
Promote short-term study abroad programs during summer or semestral breaks;
Enhance the quality of the ESL program;
Develop foreign-accredited programs to attract more international students to AUF;
Increase international initiatives and activities for AUF students, staff and faculty;
Expand various international research networks while strengthening the existing university-wide networks in interdisciplinary research areas; and
Explore other areas of cooperation such as joint international conference, collaborative research, and sharing of library holdings with projects and programs.

milestones.title: Office of Global Relations (OGR) Created
milestones.body: In 2023, to further strengthen the implementation of Internationalization initiatives and programs of the University, the Office of Global Relations (OGR) was established. The OGR is under the Office of the University President and is dedicated to provide administrative support to the academic units, enabling the strategic designing and effective implementation of internationalization initiatives and programs, contributing to achieving a competitive advantage in the field of education. Specifically, it has the following objectives:
objectives: To establish mutually beneficial partnership with international educational and research entities, institutions, agencies, and organizations, contributing to the promotion of quality education;
To provide administrative support in the planning, implementation, monitoring, and evaluation of Internationalization programs and initiatives;
To assist students, faculty, and administrators participating to Internationalization programs, both inbound and outbound;
To coordinate Internationalization activities such as school visits, benchmarking activities, and consultative conferences; and
To consistently adopt innovative practices aimed at improving the University's Internationalization programs, thereby ensuring the highest level of satisfaction among its clients.

milestones.title: Office of the Vice President for External Affairs (OVPEA) Established
milestones.body:
In March 2, 2026, to ensure harmonized establishment of networks, linkages, and partnerships with local, national, regional, and international agencies and groups to pursue its vision, mission, and goals, the University established the Office of the Vice President for External Affairs (OVPEA) which governs three units including the Office of University Relations, Alumni Affairs and Placement Services, and Confucius Institute.

The mandate of the Vice President for External Affairs (VPEA) is to provide executive-level leadership in setting the overall direction of the University’s external relations, particularly in establishing and sustaining strategic partnerships with universities, organizations, institutions, industry partners, and other stakeholders, both local and international, to support the holistic development of students, teaching personnel, and non-teaching staff. The VPEA further institutionalizes quality assurance mechanisms and data-driven systems to ensure the continuous improvement, effective monitoring, and informed management of internationalization and external engagement programs.

news.title: News & Events
news.description: Stay informed about partnership agreements, alumni initiatives, international collaborations, and other developments from the Office of the Vice President for External Affairs.

leadership.title: Leadership & Governance
leadership.intro: The Angeles University Foundation is governed by its Board of Trustees, composed of distinguished men and women from education, business, government, the judiciary, and the clergy, and is led by its President, Atty. Joseph Emmanuel L. Angeles, and the Management Committee.

president.name: Atty. Joseph E. L. Angeles, Ph.D.
president.title: University President
paragraphs: On behalf of the Angelenean community, it is my distinct honor to welcome you to Angeles University Foundation (AUF), a university committed to academic excellence, global engagement, and transformative education.

As we pursue our vision of becoming a leading center of excellence in instruction, research, and community engagement, AUF continues to expand its global footprint through meaningful and sustainable strategic partnerships. We actively collaborate with universities, research institutions, industry leaders, and international organizations around the world. These partnerships foster faculty and student mobility, transnational education programs, joint research initiatives, professional development, internships, innovation platforms, and intercultural exchange—creating pathways for shared growth and global impact.

Anchored in our enduring mission of the "Total Development of Man for God and Humanity," AUF integrates intellectual rigor with ethical formation and social responsibility. Our academic programs, duly recognized by the Philippine Commission on Higher Education (CHED), are designed to cultivate graduates who are not only academically competent but also morally grounded, compassionate, and service-oriented leaders.

Our commitment to quality and continuous improvement has earned AUF Autonomous Status from CHED and inclusion in the QS World University Rankings: Asia, affirming our standing within the regional and international academic community.

At AUF, we do not merely educate; we shape leaders, innovators, and nation-builders. We invite you to become part of a dynamic university where aspirations are nurtured, global perspectives are embraced, and excellence is a shared pursuit.
We look forward to welcoming you to AUF.

orgChart.title: Organizational Chart
orgChart.description: The Office of the Vice President for External Affairs (OVPEA) oversees three principal units to ensure cohesive strategy and implementation: the Office of University Relations, the Confucius Institute, and Alumni Affairs & Placement Services.

factsFigures.title: Facts & Figures
factsFigures.description: Angeles University Foundation maintains key institutional data and registry reports reflecting the progress and outcomes of our external partnerships and global engagements.

recognitions.title: International Recognitions
recognitions.intro: The Angeles University Foundation (AUF) actively engages in global ranking initiatives such as QS World University Rankings and Times Higher Education Impact Ranking, while also upholding international quality assurance standards through its International Organization for Standardization (ISO) 21001:2018 certification. The table below provides a consolidated summary of AUF’s performance in global rankings and its international quality assurance recognitions.

items.description: Angeles University Foundation (AUF) has been listed in the QS Asia University Rankings since 2025, underscoring its sustained efforts to achieve international recognition through excellence in teaching, research, and global engagement.

Angeles University Foundation (AUF) has been listed in the QS Asia University Rankings since 2025, underscoring its sustained efforts to achieve international recognition through excellence in teaching, research, and global engagement.

items.initiative: TIMES HIGHER EDUCATION (THE) UNIVERSITY IMPACT RANKINGS 2024
items.description: Angeles University Foundation (AUF) has participated in and been recognized by the Times Higher Education (THE) Impact Rankings since 2024, demonstrating its sustained commitment to excellence in teaching, research, community engagement, and the achievement of the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

SDG 3: Good Health and Well-being – 401-600
